judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with comparable rigor; Report 2 adds a slightly clearer two-dot vs three-dot diff distinction, but the two are essentially equivalent in substance.

report_1.evidence: The branch this work actually depends on, `feat/weighted-scorer`, is not a valid target: its content has already landed on `main` (see Q2/Q3), so targeting it would base the PR on a branch that's now redundant and could be deleted at any time. `main` is correct

report_2.evidence: Per project documentation, PRs should always target the trunk rather than stack on another feature branch as a base — which is especially important here because the branch this work was actually built on (`feat/weighted-scorer`) turns out to already be a "dead base"

report_1.evidence: its *content* already exists on `main` under a completely different, later commit: `1971f08 "Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)"`... `git diff ac9d505 1971f08 -- scoring/metrics.py` and `-- scoring/scorer.py` both produce empty output (byte-identical files)... confirming `1971f08` is a squash/rewrite, not a merge that incorporated `ac9d505` into `main`'s ancestry.

report_2.evidence: `main`'s tip, `1971f08` ("Refactor scorer for weighted metrics (#42)", Fixture Bot, 2026-06-13), has a tree hash **byte-identical** to `feat/weighted-scorer`'s tip tree hash... That's the signature of a squash-merge: the same net change landed under a brand-new, unrelated commit SHA

report_1.evidence: `scoring/scorer.py` | all +13/-3 (WEIGHTS dict, weighted `score()`) | `d6fdbc3` + `2d3029b` (W1, W2) | No ... The other 16 inserted / 3 deleted lines are the `feat/weighted-scorer` dependency's changes, present in this diff purely because `feat/pairwise-metric` was branched from that work before it was merged into `main` via a separate route

report_2.evidence: It additionally includes the entire `scoring/scorer.py` rewrite (+10/-3, 100% from `feat/weighted-scorer`'s `W1`) and 6 of `metrics.py`'s 10 insertions... This content is present in the branch not because of any error in the pairwise-metric work itself, but purely because of when the branch was forked (before the dependency merged) and the fact that it hasn't been rebased since.

report_1.evidence: Rebase off the three redundant commits explicitly: `git rebase --onto main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`... Re-run `git diff --stat main...feat/pairwise-metric` and confirm it now shows only `prompts/grader.txt` (+1) and `scoring/metrics.py` (+4)

report_2.evidence: Re-parent `feat/pairwise-metric` onto current `origin/main`, dropping the now-redundant commits — e.g. `git rebase --onto origin/main feat/weighted-scorer feat/pairwise-metric`... Re-verify: `git diff origin/main...feat/pairwise-metric --stat` should now show only the 2-file/+5 diff from Q3 — confirming the PR will review cleanly before opening it.
